    So continuing on the discussion about action tokens

  • huunguyenSat Nov 15 2025 23:41:21 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    one thing i have thought of is that a model must keep in its' memory both predicted action token, actual sensors for the joints, and then maybe actual actions?

  • huunguyenSat Nov 15 2025 23:41:46 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    like for example, an inverse kinematic system (or other system 1 model) might say "move to this position x' " where x' is not the same as predicted x

  • huunguyenSat Nov 15 2025 23:42:22 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    why don't we just change the memory? like change the prediction to actual and maybe also interleave with perception data (could be language). if we want to also record some sort of moving average error we can do taht too

  • huunguyenSat Nov 15 2025 23:42:37 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so that a model remembers what it actually did, and not what it remembed it thought it should do

  • huunguyenSat Nov 15 2025 23:43:06 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    this can be done in parrallel by editing the kv cache

  • huunguyenSat Nov 15 2025 23:44:14 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    this also has the benefit of allowing dynamic calibration

  • huunguyenSat Nov 15 2025 23:44:18 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    it's like singing

  • huunguyenSat Nov 15 2025 23:44:30 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    if you start signing - and you realize you sound off key, you adjust

  • huunguyenSat Nov 15 2025 23:44:44 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    you don't keep signing and hope you use an auto-tuner that fixes your signing

  • andreaskoepfSat Nov 15 2025 23:50:25 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Current VLAs use almost no memory at all .. I saw recently context VLA which uses an average pooling over previous image inputs.

    Took me half a day to.. but I am not a mechanic ;-). In total for the 2+2 teleop setup one needs to screw 24 servos into the 3D printed parts. First arm is the slowest because you first need to understand how it is constructer, once you understands that the cables are always on one side the orientation of the servos becomes clear etc.

  • huunguyenSun Nov 16 2025 22:29:17 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    cool - and putting it togehter is pretty fun.

  • andreaskoepfMon Nov 17 2025 17:35:55 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I think Christoph will like VLA-0 - they show that predicting actions as text is also effective (no changes to architecture): https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.13054

  • andreaskoepfMon Nov 17 2025 17:38:15 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    "Contrary to expectations from prior literature, we find that this simple formulation is highly competitive–achieving performance on par with other alternatives, while requiring no change to the underlying VLM architecture."

  • andreaskoepfMon Nov 17 2025 18:30:50 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @andreaskoepf#0 I have been thinking of action space as speech/sound data too. Dumb way is to map multi join singals and maybe sensors to singal spectrogram with some normalization and then do a VQ training to get action tokens

    sound is a time based frequency domain with interrelations and complic input output. just like joints

    i think i used the metaphor - the music of motion - or something like that. i wonder if we link dance (movment) to music b/c of this

    as humans i mean

  • huunguyenTue Nov 18 2025 01:05:44 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I think for dev it is .. for training a tokenizer it would make sense to take as many open-source datasets as possible. There are many design decisions to make. It is of course also possible to simply use FAST (which is Apache 2.0).

    yes - fast is very easy to use

  • huunguyenTue Nov 18 2025 01:16:21 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    baseline is something like - is FAST good enough. Also, I think we can use FAST as a knowledge distillation method too. [our tokens] || [fast tokens for same actions]

  • huunguyenTue Nov 18 2025 01:16:47 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i also think the issue is that there is so many different types of robotic motions - it's not all just say an arm

  • huunguyenTue Nov 18 2025 01:17:01 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    it could be rolling bots, different sizes and shapes

  • huunguyenTue Nov 18 2025 01:17:09 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    it is like multilingual audio tokenizer

  • huunguyenTue Nov 18 2025 01:17:59 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    if we were to create a tokenizer that is pretty good for many many types of action space, it could be not that good for the version we want, but it likely embeds physical knowledge and rules. (you can't pull one joint segment from another)

  • huunguyenTue Nov 18 2025 01:18:49 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    like in multilingual, where we use a base configuration (english), and pair with other types of languages to permit knowledge transfer, we can probably do same here

  • huunguyenTue Nov 18 2025 01:19:25 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    whle we train on 2 arm fixed robots on fixed base, we want the models' tokenizer mapping to generalize to humanoid arms on wheeled based for example

    Or maybe augmentation is possible - e.g. showing the scene from different angles and then let then for each view just let the model specify one of four or eight direction or predict a pixel pos .. and then combine the result sod left too front views or similar.

  • huunguyenThu Nov 20 2025 04:41:57 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Cool.

  • huunguyenThu Nov 20 2025 04:42:06 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Self consistency

  • andreaskoepfThu Nov 20 2025 04:42:07 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 Yes, using existing VLAs to generate more data for new VLAs is also a good idea.

  • andreaskoepfThu Nov 20 2025 04:44:12 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I want to collect initialization data for tasks and then further collect data via RL… but for RL to work I need a sometimes working policy to extend from the working islands towards wider distribution.

  • huunguyenThu Nov 20 2025 04:44:39 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Yup.

  • huunguyenThu Nov 20 2025 04:45:43 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i think of reinforcement learning as a way to explore the space of real movement that you can't easily sft

  • huunguyenThu Nov 20 2025 04:45:50 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    b.c the world is noisy

  • andreaskoepfThu Nov 20 2025 04:46:08 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenThu Nov 20 2025 04:46:40 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    but to do online reinforcement learning would be hard if you have it explore only to get to the right trajectory, without some demonstration

  • huunguyenThu Nov 20 2025 04:47:01 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and i have no idea what kind of RL people use in robotics

  • andreaskoepfThu Nov 20 2025 04:47:25 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • vankhuenguyenSat Feb 28 2026 17:28:51 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenSat Feb 28 2026 17:28:53 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    maybe we can do some early exit on an VL and then add another LLM tower to do action generation

  • huunguyenSat Feb 28 2026 17:30:30 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so it looks like a merged tower that forks from a VL to do LLM reasoning, and talking tower, and a tower to do action space, with the tokens then being fed back to the LLM tower

  • huunguyenSat Feb 28 2026 17:30:49 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    the fast observation, and then fast thinking of the actions

  • huunguyenSat Feb 28 2026 17:31:01 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and then the slow thinking to create long term planning, etc.

  • vankhuenguyenSat Feb 28 2026 17:40:01 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I see, that's will be good for our situation

  • vankhuenguyenSat Feb 28 2026 21:45:00 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 <@827568132444651560> the video from robot is 30fps right?

  • vankhuenguyenSat Feb 28 2026 21:48:52 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I think that we should tokenize video like this: - seed2: take first frame from every 30 frame and give out token (since the object doesn't change that much in 1 second) -> 1 group token per second - cosmos: Cosmos DV8X16X16 standard input is 8 frame, we will do 3 times 8 frame and 1 times 6 frame + 2 times duplication of last frame -> 4 group token per second - avc-lm: token per frame -> 30 group token per second

  • vankhuenguyenSat Feb 28 2026 22:55:16 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 <@827568132444651560> I'm done with the pipeline for image and video, you can see the report here for both of them

  • vankhuenguyenSat Feb 28 2026 22:55:16 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    https://docs.google.com/document/d/1g41oHZ4CYIWJYtiJljHdZRiKtwV3EKq8GqHFz7sIhQE/edit?usp=sharing

  • vankhuenguyenSat Feb 28 2026 22:55:54 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    do you think this is enough? or I have to lower the token

  • vankhuenguyenSat Feb 28 2026 23:00:52 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    for avc-lm the compression can increase if I train it on more videos and bigger vocab size, the current mini version I’m running has vocab size 4096, train on 500 videos

  • huunguyenSat Feb 28 2026 23:49:05 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @debbiey#0@andreaskoepf#0 I don’t know. You need to research it. <@719599526448463933> and <@819373883886796800> what do you think. We want faster than real time processing of the video input do we have enough speed for output.

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 01 2026 00:45:32 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    because most of the tokens are from AVC-LM and JPEG-LM

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 01 2026 00:45:41 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    we can do the architecture like you propose

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 01 2026 00:45:57 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    fast tower (early exist): 6k tokens from AVC-LM

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 01 2026 00:46:10 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    slow tower (main reasoning): 2k tokens from seed2 + cosmos

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 01 2026 00:46:52 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    also because the vision doesn't change much in some seconds, we can do something to avoid recomputing on the next context

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 00:47:32 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    don't you want to do the slow tower with smaller number of tokens? and fast tower with higher number of token?

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 00:48:55 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    re vidoe, look up how people normally do it. sometimes they create flow or delta - isn't this how AVC-LM works?

    at least the delta?

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 00:49:04 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    not sure about flow

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 00:49:42 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i think current VL part of VLA uses something like embeddings appraoch

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 01 2026 01:12:59 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I will try to reduce more token in cosmos, properly 1000 tokens but I will have to encode to see how it's look like after that (compare with the robot camera) AVC-LM work with delta (residual) and flow (motion estimation) like you said (P-frame from H.264)

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:07:48 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:08:07 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    oh that is not bad at all

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:08:12 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so here's the thing,

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:08:28 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    if you have a model that says "ah i think that is a mouse" not a "rock" that person is holding

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:08:30 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    in the slow

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:08:47 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    you can have the imager "zoom-in" to the hand

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:08:52 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    or the mouse

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:08:55 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    or rock

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:08:59 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    do you see what i mean?

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:09:19 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i don't think humans see everything in high resolution or gets the details of every object at every frame

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:10:04 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so you could for example, do a "zoom-in" to the image at a certain frame

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:10:13 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and it is an image of a mouse - you see what i mean

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:10:24 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I understand!

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:10:25 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    the assumption in reality is that things don't change form rapidly

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:10:33 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so that a mouse doesn't suddenly become a rock

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:10:36 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    or a rock becomes a mouse

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:10:51 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so you only need to know the details of the mouse ONCE

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:10:57 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and keep track of where it is afterwards

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:11:03 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    in lower resolution

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:11:15 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    in fact, a model kinda knows what a mouse looks like already

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:11:30 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so if it sees a cosmo or whatever token of the zoomed in mouse

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:11:40 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    it COULD extrapolate alot more details of the mouse in it's head

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:11:59 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    like, there could be a red laser flare, there is probably a click button, it is shaped for a hand to hold

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:12:11 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    etc. all that can be infered as text AND image - via image generator. this would be how i would generate data for training btw, for the fast-slow network

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:12:25 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    we don't even need to see the mouse from all angels basically is what i am saying

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:12:31 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    or in very high resolution detail

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:12:36 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    unless it is something unusual

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:13:33 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    "wait is that a robin flying over head"

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:13:46 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    zoom-in, no it is a "qual"

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:13:57 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    we can make a question between 2 towers slow tower ask what is that thing in the hand fast tower zoom in

  • huunguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:13:59 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i know it is flapping it's wings, i don't need to see in detail wing movements. literally this could be it's reasoning text.

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 01 2026 02:19:46 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Key Technical Concepts Motion Capture & Tokenization: Huu suggests using video analysis (identifying "stick figures," "flows," and "objects") to retroactively create action tokens from human movement. Generalized World Modeling: The goal is to create a "brain" for the robot during pretraining that isn't limited to human movement. By simulating the actions of various entities (animals, cars, etc.), the robot can extrapolate and understand movement as a universal "mode." Research & Data: Van Khue plans to review the BEAST paper and analyze datasets suggested by Andreas, noting that some already include integrated action tokens. How do we move beyond simple imitation? We aim to build a robust internal world model that allows a robot to understand and predict a wide variety of movements, potentially using animal-like simulations to improve its ability to navigate the physical world.

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 01:45:11 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @robvanvolt#0

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 01:45:48 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @spirit_from_germany#0@robvanvolt#0 <@293250279187677184> <@823812555151638538> - idk why rob wasn't in this channel. I think ties channel ties our desire to work on empathic, reasoning and embodied ai

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 01:49:11 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @spirit_from_germany#0 i talked to <@293250279187677184> about this for a long time. i think it is possible to knowledge mine many sources to create an atlas of the world knowledge or world models in graph like form, scripts/frames, etc. and to conver that back into training data. this type of knowledge includes multimodal grounded knowledge and necessarily common sense. and the missing part is action or more particularly action simulation

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 01:49:31 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    as an aside, my perspective on robotics comes from this background

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 01:49:51 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Subsumption_architecture

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 01:50:01 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    the idea is that there are many "systems"

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 01:50:18 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    the lower systems does basic things like keep an entity in a position in space - or a trajectory

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 01:50:51 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    whereas upper controllers does things blocks the lower layers, "subsumes" or refines and directs the lower layers

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 01:51:08 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    necessarily, the upper layers are smarter, more capable but slower

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 01:51:31 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    all layers have memory and invole some AI component (e.g., inferred A->B mappings)

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 01:51:54 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    this is very similar to the idea of "agents", btw. or swarm's which again, has been around for a while (back to the society of minds 😄 )

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 01:55:28 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    when i was doing my AI research in grad school i read alot about animal behaviours, and dampening

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 01:56:52 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    📎 message.txt?ex=6a38dcb4&is=6a378b34&hm=9252a1aef3d14b3e31d3c50113912046fc2c448985bcdda279cde0ea7e28f26e&

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 01:57:30 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    you can see that there are "higher level" controls that dampens or subsumes or modifies other behaviours. doesn't mean these behaviours might also be "active" but dampened

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 01:58:35 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so - if you have an overall goal of getting food, you might see immediate food, but you might dampen the control to immediately go to the food, b/c you know (or think) there is more food over the hill, and thus, you (the higher level you) cause you to go over the hill (to explore)

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:17:08 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @spirit_from_germany#0 <@293250279187677184> - pretty excited about my KG data mining for common sense relationships and persona based knowledge units - I want to extend it to physical common sense, legal common sense, etc.

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:17:31 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i like legal reasoning b/c it is very clearly defined. if we take the area of torts

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:17:57 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    things like negligence, or intentional torts we have elements that maps very clearly into our schema

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:18:01 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    for torts it is something like

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:18:10 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    1. person X acts

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:18:36 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    2. acts causes (or leads to) something (a harm to others). we call this direct cause in torts, but maybe a better term here is "leads to" for common everyday lang 3. person X has a duty of care to the others

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:19:07 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    4. There is nothing that breaks the chain of causality - we call it proximate cause (but let's just call it "cause")

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:20:02 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    5. There is some effect (like a harm) on others

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:20:28 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    For intentional torts, there has to be a state of mind. Person X "intends to do X" which maps to our Person X "wants"

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:20:40 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so Person X wants to close the elevator door

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:20:47 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Person X is a person who goes up the elevator

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:21:03 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Their closing of the elevator door causes the door to close on an old lady Person X has a duty of care as a reasonable person (think a typical common sense case of a person riding an elevator) to those in their general area, such as those entering, exiting and being inside the elevator

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:21:11 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    The old lady gets her arm broken

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:21:31 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    There's nothing in the causal chain, like the old lady has a dog pulling her inside the elevator, that breaks proxomate cause

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:21:49 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    The old lady is in fact harmed.

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:22:16 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    A criminal act goes beyond the intent to do the act that caused the harm, to the intent to actually cause the harm

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:22:34 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so in this case, Person X intends not just to close the elevator door, but to harm the old lady

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:23:54 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    then it would be a crime too, in addition to being an intentional tort

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:24:04 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so we have a hieachy, negligence, intentional tort -> crime

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:24:32 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    we also can frame alot of other legal acts, like forming a contract, or acting as a proper doctor, accountant, etc.

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 06:25:10 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    For multimodal stuff, we can feed in embeddings of video, audio and images into the system to extract KG and ultimately these persona based knowledge units

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 07:34:13 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i am able to discover false facts

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 07:34:17 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    near miss facts ...

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 07:34:17 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 07:34:33 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so we could say something like

  • huunguyenSat Mar 07 2026 07:34:53 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    "person X mistakenly believes lobster is an instance of fish" b/c the score is < 0.7

    Top small but high-performing Vision-Language (VL) models include SmolVLM (2B), Moondream2, and Qwen2.5-VL-3B/7B, which offer excellent speed and efficiency for on-device applications. These models are optimized for, or under, 7 billion parameters, with specific options like MobileVLM (1.4B-2.7B) being designed for mobile hardware. [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] Top Small & Efficient VL Models (2025–2026) • SmolVLM (2B): A highly capable model designed for speed and low memory usage. • Moondream2 (0.5B–2B): Extremely small, ideal for edge devices and fast inference. • Qwen2.5-VL (3B/7B): Provides strong multimodal understanding and competitive performance. • InternVL3-2B: Delivers high scores on benchmarks like Math-V and ChartQA. • MiniCPM-o 2.6: Offers high throughput (1000+ tokens/s) for both text and image tasks. • MobileVLM: Optimized for mobile (Snapdragon/Orin) with high tokens-per-second. • LLaVA-Phi (2.7B): A small model based on the Phi architecture for efficient, multi-modal dialogue. [1, 2, 4] Key Features & Use Cases • Performance: These models are "small but mighty," often outperforming much larger models from previous generations. • Use Cases: Ideal for on-device, mobile, or resource-constrained environments. • Capabilities: They support tasks like image captioning, visual question answering, and OCR. [1, 2, 4, 6] These models generally use a combination of a vision encoder and a lightweight language model (LLM) to achieve high performance with a small footprint. [1] AI can make mistakes, so double-check responses [1] https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f-nF-48rDIM [2] https://huggingface.co/blog/smolvlm [3] https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Kw7ZK9rliLY [4] https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/race-small-yet-powerful-vision-language-models-vlms-just-banerjee-aw6xc [5] https://www.bentoml.com/blog/multimodal-ai-a-guide-to-open-source-vision-language-models [6] https://openreview.net/forum?id=HhP9bgCugr

  • huunguyenMon Mar 09 2026 00:07:58 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Pose estimation models detect and map specific human body keypoints (joints, landmarks) in 2D or 3D from images/video, utilizing CNNs or Transformers for applications like motion tracking and gesture recognition. Popular, high-performance models include YOLO11 (fast, real-time), MediaPipe BlazePose (mobile-friendly), MoveNet, and OpenPose, with 3D estimation capabilities. [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7] Key Pose Estimation Models and Frameworks • YOLO11-pose / YOLOv8-pose: Ultralytics YOLO Docs provides cutting-edge, real-time, multi-person pose estimation, featuring variants like nano, small, medium, large, and extra-large for varied speed/accuracy needs. • MediaPipe BlazePose: A lightweight, mobile-friendly framework from Google, often used for real-time tracking on devices. • MoveNet: A fast and accurate model from TensorFlow (often used in ml5.js) that identifies 17 keypoints. • OpenPose: Known for being one of the first open-source real-time multi-person systems, offering high accuracy for detailed analysis. • HRNet: A High-Resolution Network often used in research for high-precision keypoint detection. • DensePose: Maps all human pixels to 3D surface, useful for dense, high-fidelity modeling. [4, 6, 7, 8, 9] This video demonstrates the capabilities of the YOLO11 pose estimation model: Model Approaches •

  • huunguyenMon Mar 09 2026 00:08:17 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Top-Down: Detects human bounding boxes first, then estimates poses within each box. Highly accurate for individuals but slower in crowded scenes. • Bottom-Up: Detects all keypoints in the image first, then groups them into poses. More efficient for crowded scenes. • 2D vs. 3D: 2D models provide $[x, y]$ coordinates, while 3D models (like BlazePose) provide $[x, y, z]$ to represent body parts in 3D space. [1, 5, 10] Common Applications • Sports & Fitness: Analyzing form and technique. • Healthcare: Physical therapy monitoring. • Human-Computer Interaction (HCI): Gesture-based controls. • Augmented Reality (AR): Overlaying digital assets on bodies. • Robotics: Assisting robotic, 6DoF (6 Degrees of Freedom) object manipulation. [3, 7, 8, 11, 12] Key Datasets for Training • COCO Keypoints: Widely used, featuring 17 keypoints per person. • MPII Human Pose: A standard dataset for human pose estimation. • Human3.6m: Common for 3D indoor pose estimation. • CrowdPose: Used for challenging, crowded scenes. [1, 8, 13, 14,

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 09 2026 00:38:09 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    the current state of the video tokens in every activity is

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 09 2026 00:38:35 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    in the first key frame of every second

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 09 2026 00:38:44 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    , every 8 frames

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 09 2026 00:39:11 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I will put before , every 8 frames

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 09 2026 00:39:20 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    represent the action token in that 8 frames along side the state from cosmos and avclm

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 09 2026 00:39:45 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so the VLM will learn the action before the state

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 09 2026 02:13:42 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 we can vary it by 80% forward dynamics (pose -> cosmos, avclm), 20% inverse (avclm, cosmos -> pose)

  • huunguyenMon Mar 09 2026 02:13:45 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i would vary the format.

  • huunguyenMon Mar 09 2026 02:13:58 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenMon Mar 09 2026 02:14:16 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenMon Mar 09 2026 02:14:23 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenMon Mar 09 2026 02:14:31 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 yeah we should do it

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 09 2026 02:18:49 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    dynamic task routing into 4 formats

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 09 2026 02:19:16 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    40% semantic: text + seed2 -> pose

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 09 2026 02:19:34 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    30% high level world response: text + seed2 + pose -> cosmos

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 09 2026 02:19:53 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    10% inverse kinematics: cosmos -> pose + text

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 09 2026 02:20:13 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    20% full space: text + seed2 + pose + cosmos -> avclm

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 09 2026 02:20:30 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    text, seed2, pose appear in 4 formats

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 09 2026 02:20:36 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    cosmos appears in 3 formats

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 09 2026 02:20:49 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    avclm appear in one final format, 20%

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 09 2026 02:29:38 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    we use random to decide with those probability

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:39:15 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @debbiey#0@andreaskoepf#0 <@819373883886796800> and <@719599526448463933> i have been still thinkibg about this idea of better action tokens, and in particular learning parameterized action tokens. this takes the idea of BEAST (spline tokens) a few steps further and uses modern techniques of program induction and test time training...

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:39:27 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    action tokens is necessarily a token sequence over time

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:39:48 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    but we can still think of it is just a 1D sequence like other language tokens, and unlike humans, we can keep a very very large replay buffer for robots

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:39:55 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and programming actually is a type of 1D token sequences like language. so we are really dealing with the langauge of actions here

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:40:29 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so i think we could induce a type of parameterized code for action sequences that could be generalizable and re-used as more "tokens"

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:40:30 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    https://proceedings.mlr.press/v162/duan22c/duan22c.pdf

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:40:44 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    for example, something like a sin-wave

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:41:01 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    could be induced by reading "raw" action tokens over time

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:41:18 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and then "compressed" or generlalized as a function of time, amplitude, etc.

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:41:26 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    into code called "sin(x)"

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:41:42 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    the actual token would be something like

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:42:58 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    ['TOK_123 (x,y,z)' 'x:0', '.2', 'y:0 ',.1 ', 'z:0 ',.1 ']

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:43:15 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    which in turn calls a python function that is created by the LLM itself

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:43:57 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    We would do a very small and fast lora to learn this token mapping after coming up with the python code, over generated data that the model itself creates

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:44:25 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    these functions could model inverse kinematics, include toolkits, like foruier transforms, any number of kernel functions, etc. AND of course physics engines like godot

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:44:29 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    wdyt @here ?

  • debbieyWed Mar 11 2026 07:46:09 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 Am reading the link. Would this be one of the experiments we would make?

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:46:37 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i would like to try it

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:46:43 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    there are now many VLA (action datasets)

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:46:56 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    is there a way to generlaize these into functions that a pretty good LLM can learn?

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:47:02 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i mean, we could use claude code for now

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:47:18 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    but we could then train smaller models based on many many synthetic examples over many types of action sequences

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:47:22 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    really any time series

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:50:46 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    the parameterized token generation should 1. be autonmous given our prompt - that is, operate over a period of time (maybe overnight) 2. be able to compactly model the observed data and operate quickly with minimal time and space complexity. we can make it easier for the LLM by giving it access to a physics engine like godot, isacsim, etc. we can start with something simple as a ball bouncing until it stops. 3. use reasoning over known patterns or functions to generalize or select differnt tools to use (sin, cos, splines, etc.) 4. be able to create test cases to test the funciton created. there is in math generation, a concept of generating by the LLM the next N tokens (math solution, or in this case, bouncing ball prediction), and code created by the LLM that is a solution to the problem too, and then matching whether the LLM prediction and code solution matches. another method is to do several prediction over the same problem to see if the LLM predicts the same (or similar) solution. 5. be able to create a generator to generate new data that fits the function. this could be new videos generatd from something like unity or godot. so the generator code (created by LLM) would generate many types of ball drops.

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:50:59 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and if we are brave

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:51:04 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    have the LLM

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:51:23 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    6. train a lora 7. test the lora 8. refine and go back to step 2 to fix any issues

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:52:26 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @debbiey#0 i am sure there is other liteature. we just need to search

  • debbieyWed Mar 11 2026 07:53:30 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Okay! I’ll do some searching and also look into how to train a Lora

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:53:39 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    later we can add more complexity like input->output mapping

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:53:56 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so these parameterized action tokens are reactive

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:54:08 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and not just a funciton of parameters that are hard coded in

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:54:45 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    it could be "sin(x,y,z) x=fn(camera), y=fn(joint1),", etc.

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:55:13 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    taking the idea of the subsumption architecture furhter into modern LLM

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:55:55 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    these input->output funciton could also have a hiearchy/or priority function. so that they could "run" in the background, but be subsumed by higher level function that dampens or further parameterizes the behaviour of lower functions, or be activated by events

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:56:59 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i think this is similar to the way scratch programs are created and other types of video game programmin i think

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 07:57:21 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    https://scratch.mit.edu/

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 08:03:30 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    also interesting

  • andreaskoepfWed Mar 11 2026 15:11:51 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Hm. Why do you want to use action tokens? With rectified flow you get full action chunk in very few steps. What is the idea behind parameterized tokens? What could potential benefits? Btw Do you test in simulation?

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 15:16:42 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i think we could still use recitified flow as another "simulation" which we want to match and/or another ouput we map to

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 15:17:55 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i think the benefit is that we could train directly only ouput tokens - whatever they may be to actions. the actions could be via action tokens or we take the average embedding of the action token outputs and send to methods that requires embeddings like the retified flow diffuser

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 15:18:46 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    unless i am mistaken. all we would need to train is the "head" the mapper to any actual action realizer

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 15:19:11 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    the idea behind parameterized tokens is that you can create reactive behaviours pre-emptible, and not just actions - i hypothesize it would be much faster and more generalizable than current VLA

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 15:19:57 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    we are just generating data for video tokens and starting to set up simulators now

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 15:20:03 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so we would like to test in simulation

    sort of the line from text encoder should go direclty to VL backbone

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 16:02:09 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    not to the image or video tokenizer

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 16:02:47 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and signal from average pooling goes through rectified flow

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 16:03:14 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 16:04:32 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 16:05:00 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    the basic idea is that we generate these parameterized tokens that can execute as code and it can be stateful

  • huunguyenWed Mar 11 2026 16:05:33 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    we can offload some of the thinking to these programs (read parameterized action tokens) that makes it more controllable, and understandable and deterministic

  • huunguyenThu Mar 12 2026 03:56:08 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @andreaskoepf#0 <@719599526448463933> - i was inspired by the BEAST and FAST tokenizer in coming up with the parametric/code tokenizer, which in itself is a way to create world models and action models in one

  • huunguyenThu Mar 12 2026 03:56:12 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    📎 message.txt?ex=6a38f8ac&is=6a37a72c&hm=f4d8065ff9038134e3d81563d6054f8cafed970cc5fc97272a557157ac7e79ab&

  • huunguyenThu Mar 12 2026 03:56:27 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    So we can think of this as FAST++

  • huunguyenThu Mar 12 2026 03:57:11 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=v7-RSX24CGM&t=205s

  • huunguyenThu Mar 12 2026 03:57:15 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @debbiey#0@vankhuenguyen#0 <@819373883886796800> <@631456778432937986>

  • huunguyenThu Mar 12 2026 03:58:45 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    So imagine a token that is parameterized not just to output the joint movement in the curve in the video, but as a function of weight it is holding

  • huunguyenThu Mar 12 2026 03:58:57 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    or jitter it is feeling

  • huunguyenThu Mar 12 2026 04:03:09 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    https://www.pi.website/research/fast#:~:text=To%20address%20this,language%20instruction%20following.

  • huunguyenThu Mar 12 2026 04:03:20 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @vankhuenguyen#0 <@631456778432937986>

  • huunguyenThu Mar 12 2026 04:03:33 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    they were inspired by jpeg compression to crete the FAST tokenizer

  • huunguyenThu Mar 12 2026 04:04:41 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and here is the beast tokenizer paper https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.06072

  • huunguyenThu Mar 12 2026 04:05:45 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    read also here: https://scholar.google.com/scholar?cites=5709231062843655682&as_sdt=2005&sciodt=0,5&hl=en

  • huunguyenThu Mar 12 2026 04:06:26 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    this looks like a follow up

  • huunguyenThu Mar 12 2026 04:06:26 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    https://openreview.net/forum?id=k6nTUFoqeT

  • huunguyenThu Mar 12 2026 04:09:08 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    "Our experiments with policies initialized from multiple VLMs further confirm that training a VLA on variable-length codes is substantially more challenging than on fixed-length representations. To overcome these limitations, we propose FASTerVQ and FASTerVLA, which compress action representations, reduce the effective autoregressive depth, and equip the policy with a lightweight expert pathway attuned to action-level structure. The next sections unfold these components in detail. "

  • andreaskoepfThu Mar 12 2026 04:15:29 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Re Sim use SAPIEN https://sapien.ucsd.edu/ most of the time. Currently I work most with my DK-1 robots .. I adapted RoboTwin 2.0 for it. Is of course also possible to use NVIDIA Isaac.

  • andreaskoepfThu Mar 12 2026 04:15:58 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    https://robotwin-platform.github.io/

  • andreaskoepfThu Mar 12 2026 04:23:46 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Re Beast - mostly smoothed simple quantization, DCT based FAST is interesting but needs special handling during decode - model needs to be sufficiently trained to really generate the correct number of tokens or you need to handle cases where after demonetization the coefficient matrix sizes don’t match the expected shape.

  • vankhuenguyenThu Mar 12 2026 05:45:05 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • vankhuenguyenFri Mar 13 2026 20:11:37 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I will do it like this

  • vankhuenguyenFri Mar 13 2026 20:11:52 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    for every 8 frames, we will create list of token in like this

  • vankhuenguyenFri Mar 13 2026 20:12:22 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    ... ... ... ... ... ... ... ... ... ... "

  • vankhuenguyenFri Mar 13 2026 20:13:45 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    basically the position of each part of body will have x, y normalize to [0,1] and then multiply to 255 the token will be to , to

  • vankhuenguyenFri Mar 13 2026 20:14:43 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    each part have the CONFIDENCE SCORE, we will only count it when its confidence score is > 0.5, if not the final token will be (which mean not sure, INVISIBLE) AND if there is NOTHING detected, every part will be

  • vankhuenguyenFri Mar 13 2026 20:18:11 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    1 chunk of pose token every 8 frames will have: 2 + (1 + 17 * 3) * 8 = 418 tokens/ 8 frames => APPROXIMATE 1567 tokens / 1 sec

  • huunguyenFri Mar 13 2026 20:51:07 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    > Architecture > S2 Pro builds on a decoder-only transformer combined with an RVQ-based audio codec (10 codebooks, ~21 Hz frame rate) using a Dual-Autoregressive (Dual-AR) architecture: > > Slow AR (4B parameters): Operates along the time axis and predicts the primary semantic codebook. > Fast AR (400M parameters): Generates the remaining 9 residual codebooks at each time step, reconstructing fine-grained acoustic detail. > This asymmetric design keeps inference efficient while preserving audio fidelity.

  • huunguyenFri Mar 13 2026 20:51:53 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenSat Mar 14 2026 13:00:04 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    ```import os # 1. FORCE VULKAN: Set these BEFORE importing mani_skill or sapien # This tells the engine to use your AMD GPU and ignore the search for NVIDIA CUDA. os.environ["SAPIEN_RENDER_DEVICE"] = "vulkan" os.environ["MS_RENDER_BACKEND"] = "vulkan" import gymnasium as gym import mani_skill.envs import numpy as np def main(): # 2. CREATE ENVIRONMENT # We use the Unitree G1 (Humanoid) task. # If the assets aren't downloaded, ManiSkill will attempt to do so now. print("Initializing ManiSkill on AMD GPU (Vulkan)...") env = gym.make( "UnitreeG1PlaceAppleInBowl-v1", render_mode="human", # Opens the interactive GUI window num_envs=1, # Single environment for visualization control_mode="pd_joint_delta_pos" # Easier to see movement with joint deltas ) # 3. RESET & INITIALIZE obs, _ = env.reset() # Confirming the device being used in the console try: print(f"Active Render Device: {env.unwrapped.renderer.device}") except: print("Renderer active (Manual device check skipped).") print("\nControls:") print("- Close the window to exit.") print("- Use WASD + Mouse (Right Click Drag) to navigate the 3D space.\n") # 4. SIMULATION LOOP try: for i in range(1000): # We generate a random action action = env.action_space.sample() # To make the movement look less "jittery" on a humanoid, # we apply the same action for 5 sub-steps. for _ in range(5): obs, reward, terminated, truncated, info = env.step(action) # Render the frame to your Windows GUI env.render() if terminated or truncated: env.reset() except KeyboardInterrupt: print("Shutting down...") finally: env.close() if __name__ == "__main__": main()```

  • huunguyenSun Mar 15 2026 05:33:35 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenSun Mar 15 2026 05:34:02 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenSun Mar 15 2026 05:35:22 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and then if the prediction is right that is a positive reinforcmenet signal

  • huunguyenSun Mar 15 2026 05:35:28 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and if wrong, that is a negative reinforcement signal

  • huunguyenSun Mar 15 2026 05:35:40 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    some basic instruction here ('video -> 3d human pose' tab): https://docs.google.com/document/d/1g41oHZ4CYIWJYtiJljHdZRiKtwV3EKq8GqHFz7sIhQE/edit?usp=sharing

  • andreaskoepfTue Mar 17 2026 04:48:59 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 LeRobot datasets are pretty common - at least for manipulation. Beside the videos for the different cameras (e.g. head/overview/context, left/right wrist) you get action and state signal and sometimes velocities/torque and a language instruction. actions are either joint space or Cartesian. Joints are normally in rad. For Cartesian there are different conventions for the roatsrions, e.g. quaternion or Rodrigues (3d axis, length=angle), or Euler yaw pitch roll. For Cartesian the use of axis varies, e.g. Z up or Z forward etc. - in robotics right handed coordinate system is most common. Action signal can again be different things, e.g. leader command, velocities etc. Frequency and resolution vary. Grippers are often handled a bit differently than the rest.

  • andreaskoepfTue Mar 17 2026 04:50:00 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    For training p01-p99 percentile normalization per dimension is common to map to -1, 1 …

  • huunguyenTue Mar 17 2026 04:50:27 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @andreaskoepf#0 Thank you <@719599526448463933>

  • huunguyenTue Mar 17 2026 04:51:14 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Joint space seems most simple.

  • huunguyenTue Mar 17 2026 04:51:50 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    And matches what we are simulating.

  • huunguyenWed Mar 18 2026 05:44:51 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I am wondering if we could encode both state and action in the same@vqvae.

  • huunguyenWed Mar 18 2026 05:45:15 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Btw this is similar to the way we encode speech for both hearing and speaking

  • huunguyenWed Mar 18 2026 05:46:43 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    For example if you could encode both velocity and acceleration as well.

  • huunguyenWed Mar 18 2026 05:47:10 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Then it is not a static tone. It is a speech pattern.

  • huunguyenWed Mar 18 2026 05:47:27 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Frequency, acceleration deceleration amplitude. Etc.

  • huunguyenWed Mar 18 2026 05:47:33 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    You see what I mean.

  • huunguyenWed Mar 18 2026 05:48:05 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    And of different sized persons. Of different types of agents.

  • huunguyenWed Mar 18 2026 05:52:38 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I am sure others have done this already

  • vankhuenguyenWed Mar 18 2026 05:52:55 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    yeah but look at this link, we have some very interesting technique: - Grouped Residual Quantization (GRFSQ): we group the 156 vars logically (e.g., [Position], [Vel+Acc], [Facing]) and quantize them hierarchically. This ensures we keep the good kinematics for the robot without exploding the discrete vocabulary. Also it creates discrete tokens but near smooth as continuous - Dual-Granularity and Hierarchical Tokenization (LMR), they have macro and micro tokens like our fast slow model

  • huunguyenWed Mar 18 2026 05:53:49 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Sounds good to me. Try to replicate what you have so far.

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:38:15 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    yes

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:38:18 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:38:27 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Wdyt

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:38:45 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    How many tokens for one second or action or pose

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:38:54 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Action should be smaller than pose

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:38:54 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    8 frames -> 3 discrete tokens

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:39:01 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    3 tokens -> 3 level

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:39:22 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    It’s pretty fast?

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:39:39 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    yeah I would say, I didn't try it on a whole video

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:39:43 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    let me try

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:40:17 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Ok. But think of this. An action is not a pose.

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:40:50 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    So you can start with a pose and then you do action which done over time creates the next pose.

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:41:15 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    The same action will create different posses given state.

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:41:30 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Can we encode actions too?

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:41:51 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    So that we have pose, action, pose?

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:42:21 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Action is just acceleration and velocity over time N

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:42:38 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    So not much bigger than pose.

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:42:54 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    If we have only pose we will have to do inverse kinematic

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:44:15 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    If you include a spline function you can do action over a longer range instead of linear

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:44:28 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    You see what I mean?

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:46:23 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I mean like in one state we have pos + vel + acc

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:46:31 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    if you want action only, we can do only val + acc

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:47:07 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    ah - i see

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:47:13 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so you do have action at every pose

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:47:22 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    yes

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:47:37 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so here's the thing

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:47:44 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    if you do linear projection, you get something like

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:47:56 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    arm rotating down, more down, more down

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:48:00 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    until some constraint

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:48:21 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    put you could get an action postion + action token

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:48:29 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and that is a diferent position at time X.

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:48:39 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and a different accel, velocity.

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:48:44 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    like slowing down or turning.

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:49:03 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    is this vqvae from a known method btw?

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:50:09 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    ok

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:50:10 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    here you go

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:50:17 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    📎 message.txt?ex=6a38f749&is=6a37a5c9&hm=4529f89a88f0dc151bd4af5bd7c6e1c3a6a29086ee5f4c2bb5fcd5785bb1c463&

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:50:21 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    So next step

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:50:44 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    try for each point,

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:50:49 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    have control points

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:51:08 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    which could be delta of current point

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:51:39 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so,

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:51:52 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so given current point, and next 3 points, and a spline that goes through those

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:52:05 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    time-delta could be

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:52:19 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    .1 seconds, and then .5 seconds

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:52:21 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    hetergenous

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:52:38 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so with this, you have compressed the token space even further and smoothed it out

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:52:41 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    you could STILL have

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:52:47 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    new frame with new control points

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:52:55 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    which is basically changing the point

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:55:32 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    you could do say 4 points per joint for example. one position, accel, vel, and 3 control points

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:55:57 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    you could do this kinda random in time delta

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:56:01 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so you have a person walking

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:56:11 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    you take between .1 sec to 1 sec

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:56:18 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    to get the 4 control points

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:56:47 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    the control points is a bit differnt than actual new point

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:58:47 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    arguably walking is

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:58:48 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    > Periodic Spline: Used when data is cyclic, setting equal values and derivatives at the first and last knots.

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:58:56 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    but not really

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:58:59 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    it is reactive

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 03:59:24 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 the vqvae is 1d temporal cnn autoencoder + Residual Vector Quantization (I think this appear in motionGPT, T2M-HiFiGPT)

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:05:06 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    we know that say the forearm can rotate. but at a certain point, it

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:05:10 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    has to stop

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:06:15 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    this is an additional parameter. this parameter could also be a function of another ouutput of another joint's spline.

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:06:27 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:06:31 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    yeah - we do basic first, which you just did

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:16:47 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:16:53 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:17:30 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    but still, it would be cool

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:17:43 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    to also represent as a python function for the "smart" model to understand too

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:17:58 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    the parameterized beast token is REACTIVE

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:18:42 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so, say you have a step motion that steps down a foot down - linear mostly downards with some deceleration, but you have a signal that represents tension on joint, which would create an inverse or dampening signal so that you decelerate even faster

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:18:57 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    before you could send the sensor to the LLM, then have the lLM produce a control

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:19:16 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    the parameterized beast would automatically step lighly over a rocky area

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:19:24 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    think about that!!@

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:19:50 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    we might have to fix all these parameters, like sensor, other joint's state, etc.

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:19:57 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    but again if this is all fixed

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:20:00 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    we can vqvae it all

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:20:09 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    we just need data

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:21:21 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    do you see what i mean?

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:21:30 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    we are pushing system 1 to system 0

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:21:52 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    like the way you pull away from a hot surface before you mind registers

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:22:59 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    yeah so from making an overall action like move forward, we move to reactive, low level reaction like hand back at hot surface

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:23:24 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    fast tower

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:23:46 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    well - i think it's 3 towers

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:23:51 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    tower 0, tower 1 and tower 2

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:23:59 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    tower 0 is actually not an LLM at all

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:24:17 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    it is just a bunch of control functions, represented by splines, and input, output

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:24:43 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    but the key here is that the LLM level 1 and level 2 creates these parameterized tokens (tower 0)

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:24:57 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    levels is fast and can read input fast

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:25:04 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    it can be reactive, but in some cases, it can' tbe real time. it's like qwen 1b or 3b

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:25:15 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    then there is tower 2

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:25:23 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    which is basically qwen 7b or 20b

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:25:24 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    lol

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:25:58 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    tower 2 could override the tokwn from tower 1, and it can reason and plan

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:26:10 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    tower 0: reactive, like hand in hot surface, doesn't need any thinking tower 1: intuitive, no deep logic, rather past experience tower 2: deep thinking, infer overall aim

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:26:15 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I think it's like this

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:26:28 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    yes - it is exactly like this

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:26:50 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    tower 0 are the tokens (which can be reactive if we do parameterized spline)

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:26:57 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    tower 1 is small VLA/LLM

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:27:10 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    tower 2 is biger VLA/LLM plus memory, reasoning, etc.

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:27:28 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    got it, really interesting

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:27:39 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    we're trying to replicate a pure human

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:27:42 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i think we might be able to do "early exit" on tower 2 to create tower 1

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:27:48 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    if you don't know what this is, look it up

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:27:58 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    basically, take only some of the first n -layers

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:28:14 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and we also train this on a differnet output linear

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:28:29 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so, data can come in from sensor

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:28:39 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    we can quickly create tokens from layers 1-5 for example.

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:28:54 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    but that same data output continues through the rest of the model until we have an output from the tower 2

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:28:59 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    which might change those tokens

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:29:07 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and add new tokens like thinking tokens

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:30:40 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • vankhuenguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:30:53 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenSun Mar 22 2026 04:31:09 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • andreaskoepfMon Mar 23 2026 13:34:25 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    On trajectory means here the timestamps of the samples? I think this is totally fine as you normally downsample such a tokenization approach. But is there a reason why the points are not evenly distributed in the on-trajectory example? Btw I tried BEAST also ~3 months ago: https://github.com/open-thought/qwen3-vla/tree/main/bspline_tokenizer but I have switched fully to diffusion and video models since then. Btw curious: what do you use for orientation? Euler angles, Rodrigues vector-angle, Quaternion or something else?

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 23 2026 14:53:22 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 I mean like it is nearly the same, on trajectory just a bit more

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 23 2026 14:58:51 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @andreaskoepf#0 <@719599526448463933> on trajectory here I mean the control points are actual position unlike B-spline I try curvature based sampling to better preserve the geometric shape, since uniform I think can sometimes miss important details in high curvature motions for orientation, I'm currently using the 3d position of each joints from motionbert3D but haven't modeled explicit joint orientations

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 23 2026 15:32:48 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @andreaskoepf#0 the code in your repo are really good, the current code I used for B-Spline is just experimenting, still need refinement

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 23 2026 15:53:18 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I mean like I have just thought about moving first, didn't really think about turning hands or joints something like that for orientation

  • vankhuenguyenMon Mar 23 2026 15:53:22 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 <@827568132444651560> do we need it?

  • huunguyenMon Mar 23 2026 19:15:20 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • andreaskoepfTue Mar 24 2026 00:30:29 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 it is mainly because of generalization and data efficiency .. video models learn faster with less data than VLAs - better motion priors learned from internet-scale video pre-training and IDM on top can then generalize from less samples than VLAs.

  • debbieyTue Mar 24 2026 00:47:56 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    > To adapt it for character animation tasks, we employ a modified input paradigm to differentiate between reference conditions and regions for generation.   • huunguyenTue Mar 24 2026 10:23:58 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Task Tokens: A Flexible Approach to Adapting Behavior Foundation Models | OpenReview https://openreview.net/forum?id=6T3wJQhvc3

  • huunguyenTue Mar 24 2026 10:36:48 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    The Direction task provides a reward for moving in the right direction but does not consider the humanoid’s orientation. As a result, the policy may converge to walking backwards. While this behavior achieves high reward and success metrics, it is an unwanted behavior. In Figure 5 we show that by combining human-designed priors, providing a target height and orientation for the head, the training converges to an upright forward-moving motion. An additional challenge is Strike. In this task, the agent needs to hit a target. An emergent behavior is walking backwards toward the target and then performing a “whirlwind” motion, where the agent swirls in circles to hit the target with its hand. In Figure 6 we showcase a combination of 2 prior modalities. First, conditioning on the orientation (similar to the Direction task) the agent is instructed to face the target during the locomotion phase. Then, once close to the target, the agent is guided to strike the target with its foot using a textual objective “a person performs a kick”

  • huunguyenThu Mar 26 2026 01:08:20 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    4.1 Implementation Details We use Qwen3-VL-4B-Instruct [37] as the high-level planner and SAM3 [7] to obtain the visual prompt. We use the default segmentation threshold where detection threshold and mask threshold to be 0.5. We only keep the visual prompt with highest score for target object and target location respectively. Our codebase is based on starVLA [9] framework, trained on 8 GPUs, and strictly follows the training and evaluation procedure to ensure reproducibility. We adopt QwenOFT architecture, which replace the Prismatic VLM [15] in OpenVLA-OFT [17] with Qwen3-VL-4B-Instruct. We use Qwen3-VL-4B-Instruct for System 2 Planner as well. We employ the AdamW optimizer with learning rate as 1e-5 for VLM and 1e-4 for the action model. We set the λ to be 0.1 when calculating loss.

  • huunguyenThu Mar 26 2026 01:11:14 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    "paramaterized tokens"

  • huunguyenThu Mar 26 2026 01:11:20 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    to "reactive token agents"

  • huunguyenThu Mar 26 2026 01:11:25 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenThu Mar 26 2026 01:11:25 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    lol

  • huunguyenThu Mar 26 2026 01:12:03 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    we want the model to generate tokens that are actually reactive agents that are parameterized and constrained to produce action via bsplines and reactive to inputs

  • huunguyenThu Mar 26 2026 01:12:19 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so a model can create little agents for arms,, legs, moving heads, etc.

  • huunguyenThu Mar 26 2026 01:12:28 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and then overwrite those agents with another set of tokens for arms

  • huunguyenThu Mar 26 2026 01:12:39 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so that you can do dancing, then transition to kung-fu

  • huunguyenThu Mar 26 2026 01:12:40 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    lol

  • huunguyenThu Mar 26 2026 01:12:57 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    all the while, fast and reactive to uneven surface, people getting into visual field etc.

  • huunguyenFri Mar 27 2026 06:37:42 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    While there aren't many "plug-and-play" universal walking models that will immediately balance your specific stick figure data, there are several pre-built assets and frameworks within and around ManiSkill 3 that you can adapt. 1. ManiSkill 3 "Out-of-the-Box" Controllers [1] ManiSkill provides high-level control interfaces that handle the low-level motor physics for you. Instead of writing raw torque commands, you can use: * PDJointPosController: This is the most common. You feed it your target joint angles (from your stick figure), and it uses internal stiffness and damping to move the robot's limbs. * Unitree SDK (High-Level): If using the Unitree G1 or H1 models in ManiSkill, these robots often come with a "High-Level" mode that handles balancing and basic gait internally. In this mode, you only send velocity commands (e.g., "walk forward at 0.5 m/s"), and the robot's built-in "hidden" policy keeps it upright. [2, 3, 4, 5] 2.

  • huunguyenFri Mar 27 2026 06:38:06 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Adaptation Models (Pre-trained Policies) You can leverage existing research models that have already "learned" how to walk: * HumanoidBench: This is a benchmark suite compatible with ManiSkill/SAPIEN that includes 12 locomotion tasks (walking, running) specifically for the Unitree H1 and G1. You can adapt these pre-trained Reinforcement Learning (RL) policies as a "base" and then fine-tune them to follow your stick figure's specific style. * Universal Humanoid Motion Representations: Some researchers have released "latent space" models (like those found in [Universal Humanoid Motion Representations](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.00425)) that are trained on massive motion-capture datasets. These models "know" what human-like walking looks like and can be used to "clean up" your stick figure data into physically stable motions. [6, 7, 8, 9, 10] 3. Open-Source Repositories for ManiSkill * HuSLs (Humanoid with Functional Limbs): A recently released framework (March 2026) that provides a walking gait for the Unitree H1 using imitation and curriculum learning. It includes an active dynamic balancing controller that you could potentially adapt to your own motion data. *

  • vankhuenguyenFri Mar 27 2026 06:38:08 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    AI mode is not supported in french, what do they mean :((((

  • huunguyenFri Mar 27 2026 06:38:23 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    ManiSkill Baselines: ManiSkill 3 includes tuned baselines for algorithms like PPO and SAC. You can use these to set up a "Motion Tracking" task where the robot is rewarded for matching your stick figure's positions while receiving a penalty for falling. [9, 10, 11, 12, 13] Summary Table: Which to use? | If your goal is... | Use this... | |---|---| | Quickest results | Unitree High-Level SDK (if using G1/H1). Just send "walk" commands. | | Realistic style | HumanoidBench or HuSLs models. Fine-tune their pre-trained weights. | | Custom movements | ManiSkill PPO Baselines + your stick figure as a "Reference Motion." | ------------------------------ Suggested Next Step Would you like to see a Python code snippet for setting up a basic PD Joint Position Controller in [ManiSkill](https://maniskill.ai/) to start testing your joint data on a robot?

  • vankhuenguyenThu Apr 02 2026 02:52:14 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    as you remember I tokenize a 8 frames chunk to around 204 tokens

  • vankhuenguyenThu Apr 02 2026 02:57:16 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • vankhuenguyenThu Apr 02 2026 04:07:26 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    1876819 tokens after integration, it must be more than 132,5 hours

  • vankhuenguyenThu Apr 02 2026 04:07:43 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    decode of a group of token in 8 frames

  • vankhuenguyenThu Apr 02 2026 04:08:02 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    the tokens only contain the control point, no initial position, it just illustrates the movement

  • vankhuenguyenThu Apr 02 2026 04:08:23 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I will do some more evaluations to make sure everything is fine

  • huunguyenFri Apr 03 2026 09:30:47 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I’ve been thinking about how to capture negative and positive paths and how to use those. Right now people do reinforcement learning but that requires actual robot or simulation while training. But we could try perturbing a trajectory such that some will lead to failure. Like falling down or not reaching an end pose. And we store all that as offline training. Then we can do GPRO or DPO. What do you think @here ?

    > To process large-scale, in-the-wild raw video data, we developed a fully automated data annotation pipeline comprising five modules, as illustrated in Fig. 2. The pipeline > includes (1) a video processing module that mines and extracts video clips V from noisy Internet videos, (2) a video > captioning model that generates text description of human > actions T , (3) a human pose detection module that estimates > parametric 3D human poses Phuman from video clips, (4) a

  • huunguyenFri Apr 03 2026 12:50:58 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 chatgpt and gemini now always feel empathetic

  • vankhuenguyenWed Apr 08 2026 20:41:54 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    this paper are trying to make them become an expert in feeling emotion

  • huunguyenWed Apr 08 2026 20:42:24 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    there is no full body reaction to emtions

  • huunguyenWed Apr 08 2026 20:42:48 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    but i think empathy is a naturally occuring phenomenom given the right building blocks

  • vankhuenguyenWed Apr 08 2026 20:42:55 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    yeah there are just text prompts to react, no real action to react

  • huunguyenWed Apr 08 2026 20:42:56 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    mirror neurons, and emotion feedback from actions

  • huunguyenWed Apr 08 2026 20:43:49 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i mean we could create some latent variables that represents different feelings in different situations

  • huunguyenWed Apr 08 2026 20:44:12 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and append them. so it si no instruction type emotions, but reaction to stimuli

  • huunguyenWed Apr 08 2026 20:44:24 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    basically like an image embediding

  • huunguyenWed Apr 08 2026 20:44:34 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    or literally an image embedding of a picture of a crying baby

  • huunguyenWed Apr 08 2026 20:44:45 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and the sound of a crying baby

  • huunguyenWed Apr 08 2026 20:44:53 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    or a video embedding

  • vankhuenguyenWed Apr 08 2026 20:45:29 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    yeah that should be good idea for another paper!

  • huunguyenWed Apr 08 2026 20:45:30 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    we don't train the model to "know" what that embedding is. it just is triggered in the write social situation

Toyota says testing that kind of learning in a live environment is a key part of the project. "We believe it is an exceptionally valuable opportunity to validate a reinforcement-learning-based robot in the inherently uncertain environment of a basketball arena," Tomohiro Nomi, Head of Humanoid Robotics Research Unit, Frontier Research Center, Toyota Motor Corporation, told CyberGuy. "Moving forward, we will continue developing robots that inspire and bring joy to people."

  • huunguyenThu Apr 30 2026 01:59:38 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    📎 message.txt?ex=6a38dd5a&is=6a378bda&hm=544a11a71f46089b7d821a3e50c6a5d9f2fe149339a5e6186d66fedd230406ac&

  • huunguyenThu Apr 30 2026 02:00:06 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    ```import numpy as np def compress_spline_trajectory(spline_coeffs, threshold=0.002): """ Compresses trajectory by freezing stationary B-spline control points. Args: spline_coeffs: Array of shape (num_control_points, num_joints, 3) threshold: Euclidean distance change in the coefficient space """ num_cp, num_joints, _ = spline_coeffs.shape # 1. Local Frame Transformation (Pelvis-Relative) # Using the Pelvis (index 0) as the global movement driver pelvis_coeffs = spline_coeffs[:, 0, :] relative_coeffs = spline_coeffs - pelvis_coeffs[:, np.newaxis, :] # 2. Coefficient Delta Check # We check if the control point itself has moved significantly # compared to its position in the previous spline segment. cp_diffs = np.linalg.norm(np.diff(relative_coeffs, axis=0), axis=-1) # 3. Create a Sparse Update Mask # True = Control point is 'moving' and needs a new value # False = Control point is 'stationary' and can be cached/ignored update_mask = np.ones((num_cp, num_joints), dtype=bool) update_mask[1:] = cp_diffs > threshold return relative_coeffs, update_mask```

  • huunguyenThu Apr 30 2026 02:00:08 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    ```[AGENT] exploration agent [GOAL] Walk to Gate B12 and monitor for spills. [VISION] [AGENT_MONITOR] list_active_agents(context="airport_humanoid_vla") {"active": ["MotionAgent", "SafetyAgent", "VisionAgent", "SystemAgent", "ExplorationAgent (self)"], "status": "nominal"} [NARRATIVE] Initializing cyclic gait. The upper body is locked relative to the pelvis. Sub-agents are running in parallel to monitor for environmental hazards and safety constraints. [TIMING] [PHASE] 0.0 -> 1.0 (Cyclic) [MAX_CYCLES] 50 [STEP_DURATION] 1.1s [AGENT_COMMUNICATION] Priority 1 (Safety): message_to_agent(safety, "[COLLISION_PROXIMITY] < 0.3m -> GOTO [STATE_EMERGENCY_STOP]", priority=1) {"response": "ok"} Priority 2 (Goal): message_to_agent(vision, "IF [VIS_MATCH] == 'spill_detected' -> SIGNAL [STATE_CLEAN_TRANSITION]", priority=2) {"response": "ok"} Priority 3 (Boundary): message_to_agent(monitor, "IF [CYCLE_COUNT] >= 50 -> SIGNAL [STATE_REPLAN_PATH]", priority=3) {"response": "ok"} [ACTION: STATE_WALK] [ACT_START] [PELVIS_CP_0] <+0.05, +0.00, +0.02> @ t=0.0% [LOOP] [L_LEG_CP_1] <-0.12, +0.01, -0.05> @ t=25.0% [LOOP] [R_LEG_CP_1] <+0.12, +0.00, -0.05> @ t=50.0% [LOOP] [L_LEG_CP_2] <-0.08, -0.02, +0.10> @ t=75.0% [LOOP] [ACT_END]```

  • andreaskoepfThu May 21 2026 14:49:28 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 Yes, agree. Therefore I would like to quantify this a bit .. the normal video data is not necessarily physically plausible and it is most of the time single cam mono-ocular. There are a number of experiments like combining multiple views, adding a (synthetic) depth channel etc. Seeing the video prediction performance on an eval set with unseen object positions from context start frame or etc. would be very informative. Adding cartesian action heads also easily possible.

  • huunguyenThu May 21 2026 14:50:33 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Agree on doing this. There are depth estimation models you can use to add synthetic data.

  • huunguyenThu May 21 2026 14:51:45 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)
