  • andreaskoepfThu May 21 2026 15:11:09 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    re lingbot - what I like about their engineering approach is that they re-sampled extracted VAE as separate preparation step, e.g. for robotwin they use a T-shape setup with headcam above left and right wristcams below .. but they don't actively decode video during training and prepare these frame compositions in pixel space (as I naively always did before) but they concat the VAE latents into this T-shape .. which probably also has some advantages because you don't have this cross-view interference you get when encoding a combined view.

  • andreaskoepfThu May 21 2026 15:15:19 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    btw higher level planning .. after trying (struggling) a lot with accurate text descriptions of lego duplo assembly steps I now firmly believe that for many applications text simply sucks - you really feel the "an image is worth a thousand words" ;-). Therefore I like the sub-goal image approach used by PI. Would also recommend conditioning on sub-goal image instead of only text description.

  • huunguyenThu May 21 2026 15:21:32 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Can you have the model imagine subgoal images. Conditions on current video so far and actuator history and text planning ?

  • andreaskoepfThu May 21 2026 21:29:52 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 Yes, I think so .. simplest would be LLM generated prompt with a current frame as input (e.g. if target is visible). The higher<->lower, planning<->execution interface is a large design-space - experiments have to show what really works.

  • andreaskoepfThu May 21 2026 21:31:44 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 I could prepare a bit of training code and run some smaller scale preliminary experiments. I am not sure how oftenChristoph is reading this DM-group .. would be great if you could ping him elsewhere.

    parametric spline agents

  • huunguyenFri May 22 2026 03:56:09 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    PSA

  • huunguyenFri May 22 2026 03:56:10 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    lol

  • huunguyenFri May 22 2026 03:56:18 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    idk - some acronym that is cool

  • huunguyenFri May 22 2026 03:57:06 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    PRISM (Parametric Robotic Interpolated Spline Modules) Vibe: Clean and implies a spectrum of capabilities (like light through a prism). OSMOS (Omni-Spline Multi-agent Operating System) Vibe: Sounds like "Osmosis," implying a natural flow of data and action. PHANTOM (Parametric Hermite Agents for Neural Trajectory Omni-Modals) Vibe: High-tech and slightly mysterious.

  • huunguyenFri May 22 2026 03:57:13 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    The "Functional & Research" Picks P-SOMA (Parametric Spline-based Omni-Modal Agents) Vibe: "Soma" means body in Greek, which fits perfectly for robotics and physical agents. FAST (Flexible Agentic Spline Trajectories) Vibe: Directly plays into the "Fast/Slow" thinking aspect. M-PISA (Multi-agent Parametric Interpolating Spline Agents) Vibe: Academic and easy to cite.

  • huunguyenFri May 22 2026 03:57:19 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    The "Dual-Process" (Fast/Slow) Specifics CHIASM (Cubic Hermite Interpolated Agentic System Modals) Vibe: A chiasm is a crossing or intersection, perfect for the intersection of System 1 (fast action) and System 2 (slow planning). GLIDE (Generalized Latent Interpolated Dual-engine Executors) Vibe: Suggests the smooth, non-oscillatory motion that PCHIP provides.

    @andreaskoepf#0 <@719599526448463933> the interleaving is emperical - we need to figure it out action/observation/planning/thinking interleaving. it could be that there are actually parallel agents running in parallel. we don't know yet. but i think flattening out might be easier for our training. We could flatten out our actual dataset and then just mask for different agents. So an observer agent sees alot more observation, but less action etc. Same data permuted in different ways for different "agent" view of the model And at inference, we run several of the same model with different system prompt and data streams

  • huunguyenFri May 22 2026 04:01:28 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    And they all work together

  • huunguyenFri May 22 2026 04:03:05 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    the speaking agent sees alot more orpheuis tokens

  • huunguyenFri May 22 2026 04:03:11 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    but all agents see some set of everything

  • huunguyenFri May 22 2026 04:03:21 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so our AI can talk, see, plan, walk

    > 1. The On-Device Range: 80M – 250M Parameters > For modern edge applications (phones, browsers, or local CPU servers), a high-quality "neural" voice is now achievable with surprisingly small models. > > Low End (~80M - 100M): Models like Kokoro-82M and Gradium Phonon (~100M) are the standard for high-performance, lightweight TTS. These models can run in-browser or on a single CPU core while maintaining a Word Error Rate (WER) as low as 1.48%. > > Mid-Range (~230M - 250M): Models in this category (e.g., NeuTTS Nano) offer a balance, providing slightly better language handling than the 100M tier without the heavy compute requirements of larger models.

  • huunguyenSun May 24 2026 04:07:54 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenSun May 24 2026 04:08:11 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    BUT after some pretraining we remove the tokens we don't use from the vocab. say all the jpeg tokens.

  • huunguyenSun May 24 2026 04:08:16 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and we continue training without those.

  • huunguyenSun May 24 2026 04:08:35 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • andreaskoepfMon May 25 2026 14:20:56 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 hm - it would be wise imho to make a PoC and show that the captured data in principle is accurate enough to be useful. I worked on some tasks which require sub-cm precision and it is surprisingly hard to get VLA to perform well on them. Even being off by 0.1 rad on shoulder or elbow leads to mis-grasps etc.

  • andreaskoepfMon May 25 2026 14:25:55 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 But running a sim or eval is not a real problem - training a model that performs well in simulation (or the real world) on many tasks and also outside its exact training distribution is. If you look for example on X at the countless MolmoAct zero shot videos … we are still pretty far away from truly out of the box working solutions - even with the same robot type it was trained on…

  • andreaskoepfMon May 25 2026 14:28:15 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Like this for example: https://x.com/vruga_/status/2058725128184439248 I mean .. yes, very cool that it moves somewhat into the right direction … but still SOME work is required until we get the for wide range of tasks working zero-shot promptable policies.

    @andreaskoepf#0 <@719599526448463933> - i think we should think and research on this aspect. How do we get high precision, high OOD generalization, w less data? I think looking at humans and animals important. Your experience with actual robots is soo cool 😄 Thank you for sharing! ===== Robotics maps this human process through a field called active vision or active perception, where a robot dynamically controls its cameras to focus computational power only on critical areas, rather than processing an entire high-resolution video stream at once. This biological architecture solves the "data bottleneck" problem in robotics, mapping to specific hardware and software systems: ## 1. Foveated Rendering and Dual-Camera Systems (The Fovea) * Dual-Camera Arrays: Robots use a wide-angle peripheral camera to capture global context and a secondary, high-resolution pan-tilt camera (the fovea) to lock onto specific objects. * Foveated Imaging: Software dynamically reduces the resolution of the outer edges of a camera frame. It keeps only the central target pixel cluster at 100% resolution, saving massive amounts of processing power. ## 2. Saliency Maps and Attention Mechanisms (Saccades) * Predictive Scanning: Robots do not scan a room randomly. They use AI algorithms called "saliency maps" to identify high-contrast edges, sudden movements, or specific shapes (like a door handle or a human face). * Next-Best-View (NBV): The robot’s computer brain calculates the "next-best-view" and physically jerks the pan-tilt camera actuator to focus on that spot, mimicking a human saccade. This is a RL prediciton problem btw ^ @here

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 16:33:38 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    ## 3. Visual SLAM and Egomotion Suppression (Stitching & Suppression) * Visual SLAM: As the robot's camera moves, Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (vSLAM) algorithms stitch the high-resolution glimpses into a static, 3D digital map of the environment. * Egomotion Compensation: Just as the human brain suppresses blur during eye movement, a robot uses its Internal Measurement Units (IMUs) to ignore camera feed distortion caused by its own motors turning. ## 4. Neural Radiance Fields & Generative AI (The Mind's Eye) * NeRF and 3D Gaussian Splatting: When a robot looks away, it "reconstructs" the room in its memory using technologies like Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs). It turns a few sparse high-resolution snapshots into a continuous, 3D mental model. * Predictive Fill-In: Modern robots use generative neural networks to predict what the hidden backside of an object looks like, filling in missing physical data using past "experiences" just like human memory. And for this, we should video generaiton models ^ @here If you want to explore further, let me know if you would like to focus on: * The hardware (pan-tilt cameras, foveated sensors) * The algorithms (vSLAM, saliency mapping) * A specific application (autonomous driving, humanoid grasping)

  • blanchon.jlMon May 25 2026 22:39:02 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Using LLM as a starting point for robotics is quite inefficient, 95% of the knowledge they have is useless when applied to robotics. I think the robotics modality should be consider as first class citizen, and we should basically leverage other foundational model to eventually create synthetics data but not as a starting point

  • blanchon.jlMon May 25 2026 22:40:25 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    There is also a lot of work in done in repurposing video model into robotics (typically Fast-WAM). It's kind of the opposite of VLA, using WAN as a starting point

  • blanchon.jlMon May 25 2026 22:44:23 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    On this subject in general I found it almost impossible to do proper research and ablation. Mainly because data in robotics is quite hard to get, it's also quite hard to benchmark. And right now the task we are doing with such policy are soo basic that we more or less only benchmark the proprioception and physical understanding of the model and not really the long term planning of it and pure "intelligence". Hopefully robotics proprioception would be solve soon by just scaling data and training, I don't think it's a bit challenge. However having a policy that actually can take smart decision, learn in few shot, recover from failure ... is way harder, and most paper doesn't really seems to have got there as we are still stuck on proprioception and basic tasks

  • blanchon.jlMon May 25 2026 22:44:42 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    So I build this dataset for this specific usecase (among other usecase): https://blanchon-opencs2-dataset-viewer.hf.space/

  • blanchon.jlMon May 25 2026 22:45:01 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    You guys can have a look and play with the viewer

  • blanchon.jlMon May 25 2026 22:47:52 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I haven't work on training any VLA (or other kind of policy/action model) for now, but this is definitively something that would be very interesting if you want to. What's interesting is the input space is simpler, as well as the main dynamics (learning to move around ...), and most of the learning to get is actually on higher level of intelligence: strategy, recover from failure, multi agent collaboration ...

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:49:45 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    > There is also a lot of work in done in repurposing video model into robotics (typically Fast-WAM). It's kind of the opposite of VLA, using WAN as a starting point

I think the robotics modality should be consider as first class citizen, and we should basically leverage other foundational model to eventually create synthetics data but not as a starting point

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:50:37 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    We are pretrainin robotics models so it is all interleaved. To get good world reasoning into the LLM space and good planning into the robotics space

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:50:43 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    https://huggingface.co/EmpathicRobotics

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:51:01 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    And we are going to borrow other data for emotion projection and detectiion.

  • blanchon.jlMon May 25 2026 22:51:33 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Look like a bit the approach of nvidia cosmos

  • blanchon.jlMon May 25 2026 22:51:58 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:52:19 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    yes - and we will do early exit to do physical stuff at lower layers

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:52:24 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so that we get fast action

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:53:47 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    our current plan

    📎 pab_spline_vla_spec_2_1.pdf?ex=6a38b1cb&is=6a37604b&hm=3dbcae32fd956d7a64ca1b96a117fb11924d61ee5d10f2252e0ec2618169386d&

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:54:07 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    we do tokenize using the cosmos tokenizer, among other tokenizers

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:54:24 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i studied and did robotics many years ago when i was doing my phd (dropped out and only got my masters)

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:54:51 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    at the time, we worked on this idea of a subsumption architecture - where each layer is completely capable of input -> ouput

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:54:59 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    but higher and higher layers subsumes the lower

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:55:06 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Now we call this "agents" 😄

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:55:36 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    > On this subject in general I found it almost impossible to do proper research and ablation. Mainly because data in robotics is quite hard to get, it's also quite hard to benchmark. And right now the task we are doing with such policy are soo basic that we more or less only benchmark the proprioception and physical understanding of the model and not really the long term planning of it and pure "intelligence". Hopefully robotics proprioception would be solve soon by just scaling data and training, I don't think it's a bit challenge. However having a policy that actually can take smart decision, learn in few shot, recover from failure ... is way harder, and most paper doesn't really seems to have got there as we are still stuck on proprioception and basic tasks

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:56:20 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I think the issue with proprioception is data. We need more of it, but focused. I don't think we can get it just from observation. It would need to be gotten from actual robot moving around.

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:57:12 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    But you need a continous learning loop - basically improve the vla (short hand for omni action) every few hours while it "sleeps" with maybe some memory adjustment and agenitve.

    Maybe better parameterize the sense->action loop

  • blanchon.jlMon May 25 2026 22:57:57 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:58:05 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    very cool 😄

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:58:34 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:58:40 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    but it can still do stuff in the short term.

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:58:54 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Babies don't gestate trying to learn 1Ms of iterations of running in the womb

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 22:59:06 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 23:00:17 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    So we are buildig the foundation - a small 2B model, with early exit at say 200M parameter, to do things like speech synthesis, action output, etc. with function calling to a "balance" program that is hardwired to keep the robot from falling over at the early exit, and continued onto the 2B whole model for planning

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 23:00:45 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    A video generation capability to medium term "plan" - > basically medium term dreaming

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 23:00:57 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    All these things we can do today and we have been working on.

  • huunguyenMon May 25 2026 23:01:26 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Empathic Robotics - Omni-Action

  • andreaskoepfTue May 26 2026 18:30:40 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @blanchon.jl#0 Nice paper.

  • andreaskoepfTue May 26 2026 20:21:11 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @blanchon.jl#0 <@471670179739467787> what do you think about https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.20752 or 3D for world models in general?

  • blanchon.jlTue May 26 2026 20:27:07 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I don't know this paper it's quite recent. But in general I feel like 3d reconstruction is a good idea for generating environment for robotics. Expecially that it allow post processing and having human double check ... so even if the initial results is not perfect you can always have a little bit of human work to make it suitable

  • blanchon.jlTue May 26 2026 20:29:21 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    One big problem with 3dgs environment would be the physical aspect in simulation. You wont be able to compute contact force against something that is as spiky as a gaussian splat, it will make the simulation unstable and it's also very far from what you get in real life. So it's good visually but not good physically. You can try to meshify it and do marching cube for example, but you still end up with not very realistic geometry (typically a table will not be flat enought)

  • blanchon.jlTue May 26 2026 20:29:37 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    But today it's clearly the most promising approach we have

  • blanchon.jlTue May 26 2026 20:31:16 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I'm super mad right now, yesterday the GPU machine I was using was suddently shutdown. I went complaining at the support of the cloud provider. They did a routine "maintenance" on the machine without any warning, and while my VM was trying to launch again, someone else outrun me a get my machine before me. And for the last 15h I didn't get any supply back

  • blanchon.jlTue May 26 2026 20:31:35 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    So basically you pay on demand price but you get something that look like spot 😢

  • blanchon.jlTue May 26 2026 20:32:30 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    This is really crazy behavior, at the rate it's going I'm kinda afraid open source/open research will died soon just because big labs are taking all the supply

  • blanchon.jlTue May 26 2026 20:48:50 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    World model for counter strike, I'm tokenizing video as discrete token with a new loss that make the latent more predictable for an autoregressive next chunk of frame prediction model. And also avoid error accumulation. It was starting to work really well, before my machine got killed lol

    High-quality recording of a middle-aged male voice actor. He sounds deeply vulnerable and filled with an almost childlike, giddy excitement as he unpacks and assembles a brand new chainsaw. His voice is bright, breathless with anticipation, and filled with genuine, innocent joy. with genuine delight: "Oh man, would you look at that... absolutely gorgeous. Come here, let's get this chain seated. Yes! Oh, I can't wait to try this out!" The actor lets out an eager, delighted chuckle. Suddenly, The actor's performance immediately fractures into raw, unimaginable physical agony. PAIN!! HORROR!!! SHOCK!!! He erupts into a deafening, blood-curdling shriek of pure, primal pain. There are no words spoken—only violent, frantic gasps for air between tearing, guttural screams. He chokes on his own breath, sobbing and wailing hysterically in absolute, blinding desperation and shock as the recording abruptly ends. Natural realistic genuine spontaneous performance

  • vankhuenguyenThu May 28 2026 02:34:02 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    When you see the sequence inside 128 200 55 ... : - Each number represents a quantized coordinate mapped directly to the vocabulary embeddings. - The sequence follows a strict, non-negotiable structural order: 4 PCHIP control points * 17 joints * 3 coordinates (X, Y, Z) = exactly 204 integer numbers.

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 02:34:16 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • vankhuenguyenThu May 28 2026 02:35:52 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    - In the raw JSONL string: To keep the file size clean, the script saves them as integer strings 128 200 55 inside the wrapper. - When loading via Hugging Face Tokenizer: Our custom tokenizer setup automatically catches these integers and maps them directly to the unique atomic Token IDs (, ) in the expanded vocabulary! So when you do model.generate(), the model WILL output tokens like , not raw text numbers.

    ```from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer # Replace with the path where you downloaded the model via hfd model_path = "EmpathicRobotics/vla-1.7b-pab-spline-25b-test" # Load the tokenizer and model from your local directory tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_path) model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained( model_path, trust_remote_code=True, device_map="auto" # Automatically offloads weights to GPU(s) if available ).half().cuda() #print (tokenizer.vocab) print ( tokenizer.convert_ids_to_tokens(model.generate(**tokenizer(["A man is walking down the street. 5"], return_tensors="pt").to(model.device), max_new_tokens=50)[0])) ```

    Please walk through exactly what you are doing in this channel

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:48:58 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    when you do it

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:49:25 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @vankhuenguyen#0 So we minimize confusion next time <@631456778432937986>

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:49:28 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    and create a working document

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:49:36 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    to be super clear what I am looking for: Stage 1 - minimal PoC model

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:49:46 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    1. Just save everything as tokens in text form like this

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:49:52 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:50:06 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    where you have many types of token. So not literally the word "tok"

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:50:12 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:50:21 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    etc.

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:50:30 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Interleaved with human text

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:50:53 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    JUST save everything as a a bunch of text strings in hf dataset.

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:51:12 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    2. Add the new tokens to your vocab. so add "": 24343, etc.

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:51:43 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    3. Tokenize everything so you produce a .bin/.idx which are just integers for each token. Make sure the "" makes ONE token

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:51:50 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    4. For the minimal test we also need some english data and other multimodal data besides VLA. Otherwise we get extremely long samples of only 1-2% english text followed by just omni tokens.

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:52:13 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    List all dataset you use to train. We have th mv-omni, stack-exchange-images. Those are MINIMAL. Add those.

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:52:25 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    otherwise the model won't know any english.

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:52:46 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Add also, some minimal portion of MV-1. you have already this dataset. It is what you called the "base" dataset.

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:52:59 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    5. Train

  • huunguyenThu May 28 2026 23:54:37 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @debbiey#0 Train say on 100B tokens. Stage 2. Eval on basic language evals and MS COCO Caption and VQA. Maybe extend this to video evals too. For VLA tokens, <@819373883886796800> and I will eval it. Gold test is if the output can actually drive Maniskill or Mujoco. Stage 3. - The real reserach I wanted to do. Parametgerized cycle Spline tokens. So we compress the VLA tokens to be much shorter and more efficient. Stage 4. Eval this new model

  • huunguyenFri May 29 2026 00:02:00 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    ```### global_context: A video analysis of Henri Rousseau's painting "Scouts Attacked by a Tiger" hosted by Nancy Ireson . The video explores the painting's artistic elements, historical context, and relevance today. ### scene_title: Introduction to the Painting ### scene_thematic: Art appreciation, virtual museum tour. ### scene_mood: Curious. The scene starts as Neutral/Content and transitions to Curious with visual shift. ### text_prompt: Nancy Ireson talks about the painting she wants to discuss. ### speech_transcript: Hello, my name is Nancy Ison, I'm the Guns Family Chief Curator at the Bounce Foundation , and you're joining me in my home this morning, but really I'd like to be taking you on a visit to the Bounce Foundat ion and I'd like us to head up to Gallery 14, which is on the second floor of the gallery, and it's just a wonderful sp ace. It's a little more intimate than the main floor, the ceilings are lower, and you see this wonderful horizontal arr arrangement that I found at Dr. Bans created. Now, the work that I'd like us to focus on is at the centre of the wall, an d you can see in this arrangement how the composition itself really helps to focus our eye, despite everything that's g oing on around it, the decorative arts, the paintings, the metal work, we can't help but be attracted to this really bi g, giling jungle scene that just captures our attention so strongly, and let's leap in. So here we have it. It's called Scouts Attack by a Tiger, ### video_tokens: ```

    this is let's say, step 0 to get the VLA/omni model to work then next we need to 1) connect to maniskill or mujoco,, and 2) generalize the action tokens to parameterized cyclic spline tokens We also have alot of omni data - all part of the original MV project (as is the finevideo-vla) MV-Omni has image, snac, seed2 tokens

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 03:30:38 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so we could mix in mv-omni, mv-base subset (1 or 1.5) and finevide-vla

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 03:31:42 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    then after we can be certain the model produces somewhat coherent action tokens (even if it is prediciting actions that are bad - that makes the robot fall over), we can filter/augment by feeding through maniskill to upsample the data with more action tokens.

  • andreaskoepfSat May 30 2026 03:37:55 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I like that Qwen-VLA specifies embodiment icnl. cams and control frequency in the prompt. They trained on various action types, delta EEF with euler angles, Abs joints and some humanoid variants .. single and dual arm. Seems they used mostly q01-q99 quantil normalization.

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 03:39:07 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    interesting. we have timing we could put in prompt. we don't have real motors so can't send frequency

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 20:45:48 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    you mean they didn't move?

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:46:03 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    where the trajectory does not need any more control points

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:46:09 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    you keep the points anyway?

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:46:26 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so it ends up to be exactly 204 data points per 8 seconds?

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 20:46:33 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    yes

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:46:37 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    lol

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 20:46:38 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    it will be more when I add the anchor

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:46:52 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so - the point of the spline is to reduce the number of points

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:47:17 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    since we know at time 1 it is xyz. and time 3 it is abc, and then finally at time 8 it could be qrs

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:47:25 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    the spline travels through these points

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:47:32 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so for that point you only keep 3 points

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:47:52 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    each joint could have up to N points. but some might have just two

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:48:02 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    do you see what i mean?

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:48:06 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    it is compression

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:49:00 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so i understand that it is exactly the same points as if we didn't use splines

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:49:10 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    but it just so happens it is mapped to movement using spline right?

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:49:21 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so if we turned off spline interpolation, it would be jerkey?

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:49:33 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so spline usage is for rendering, not compression?

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:49:40 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    am i missing something?

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:50:59 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 20:52:18 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i understand the point of compression that you said, what's I did was just trying to put 4 control points for every 8 frames to ensure smoothness of movement and also the alignment of num tokens put into training

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:52:36 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    that's fine

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 20:52:39 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 20:55:31 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so for example you have the point on 8 frames of 1 joint, I used PCHIP to extract the control points which can be used to interpolate that movement later I saved everything in the files so the data is still there (we can resume any phase easily)

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 20:56:13 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    movement of joint -> control points -> tokens (which is saved in the current dataset) -> decode -> control points -> PCHIP interpolation -> movement of joint with good precision to put in maniskill

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:56:21 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    is it in the dataset?

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 20:56:46 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    yes, the movement is then quantize into the agent tokens, which need the decode function

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 20:56:56 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    I also saved everything before encoding in a folder

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 20:57:04 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so that I can rerun again if wrong

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 20:57:10 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 21:07:54 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 frequency of tokens created: - seed2: 1 per second - cosmos, agent, avc-lm: 1 per 8 frames (30fps videos) for each joint movement in an 8 frames chunk, we convert to only 4 control points which can be interpolated to the initial movement

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:08:17 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so we have alot more seed2 data than we actually put in the dataset

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 21:08:21 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 yes you can try that, last time I did uniform 4 control points

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:08:33 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    i know

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:08:34 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    lol

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 21:08:37 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 seed2 is like, 32 tokens for every seconds

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:08:49 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    just to repeat

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:09:01 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    >, last time I did uniform 4 control points

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:09:05 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:09:13 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 21:09:45 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    the raw data I'm going to push is just 3d location of joints in the video spline created 4 control points and not uniform in time (which is adaptive I put it in the code)

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:10:20 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    ok. we were just missing time. but that adds a 4 dimension

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:10:20 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    lol

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 21:10:38 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    to be particular, in a 8 frame chunks: - 1st control points: 1st frame location - 2nd, 3rd control points: adaptive - 4rd control point: last frame location

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:10:59 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    2nd and 3 is adaptive. what do you mean?

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:11:06 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    it is actual points on the line right

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 21:11:15 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    yes on the line but decide by PCHIP

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:11:15 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    but the timing is not uniform

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 21:11:21 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    the timing is not uniform

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 21:11:33 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    because sometime the movement is suddenly change during the 8 frame chunks

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:11:34 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    yes - PCHIP just projects and chooses something

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:11:42 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    yeah - i know

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 21:11:47 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so uniform time will like not good

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:12:08 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    so really there is a tradeoff betwen number of control points given we increased the dimension y 1

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 21:12:40 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    yes, more control points, better reconstruction, if u have time can u ask claude if we can make num control points flexible

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:38:24 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Raw MotionBERT output (8 frames × 17 joints × 3 xyz) ↓ Relative to pelvis joint_rel = joint_xyz - pelvis_xyz pelvis = [0,0,0] = free ↓ Variable control points per joint fit PCHIP per joint individually use as many CPs as needed (1-8) store (x,y,z,t) per CP ↓ Flatten to token stream x y z t x y z t ... x y z t x y z t x y z t x y z t ... ↓ BPE over full corpus frequent xyzt patterns → single tokens rare/complex motion → stays verbose ↓ Final block — variable length, lossless

  • vankhuenguyenSat May 30 2026 21:39:08 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:42:42 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Yes! This is exactly the right intuition. BPE on code is the perfect analogy: --- ## Just like code BPE In code, `for i in range(len(` appears so often it becomes a handful of tokens or even one. Same idea here: ``` Walking cycle (repeating every ~1 second): ... → BPE merges to → ``` --- ## Your examples are exactly right **Stationary body:** ``` x y z ← 1 CP, whole chunk = 4 tokens → BPE → ← 1 token ``` **Cyclic motion (stirring, waving, walking):** ``` xyzt xyzt xyzt xyzt xyzt xyzt ← repeating pattern → BPE → ← 1 token ``` **Whole body walking:** ``` ............ → after enough BPE rounds → ← entire lower body motion = 1 token ``` --- ## The deeper insight BPE naturally discovers **motion primitives** from data without any supervision: - `` — joint not moving - `` — pendulum motion - `` — quick flex - `` — common manipulation primitive - `` — full gait These aren't hand-designed — they emerge from frequency in the corpus. Exactly like how BPE on code discovers `function`, `return`, `for i in range` as natural units. --- ## And it composes Just like code tokens compose into programs, motion tokens compose into actions: ``` = "walk to table, pick up object, carry it, put it down" ``` That's the action representation your VLA model would learn to predict. Not 204 floats — a handful of meaningful motion tokens per activity. --- ## This is actually novel FAST uses VQ for this. Nobody has applied BPE directly to variable-length xyzt joint trajectories as far as I know. This could be the core contribution of the NeurIPS paper.

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:43:08 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    claude thinks it is a good idea for a neurips paper.

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:43:15 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:43:19 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:45:36 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    also, i would reorgnize so it is pelvis, and then hands, and then feet, and then other parts of body

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:47:26 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    xyzt xyzt xyzt ← predict first, most detail xyzt xyzt xyzt ← predict second xyzt xyzt ← third xyzt xyzt ← fourth xyzt xyzt ← fifth, follows wrist xyzt xyzt xyzt ← follows ankle xyzt xyzt ← compensates for elbow/wrist xyzt xyzt ← whole body compensation xyzt xyzt ← last, global anchor xyzt ← gaze follows task xyzt xyzt xyzt

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:48:06 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    > This is essentially learned inverse kinematics — the model discovers IK from data because the token order forces it to reason end-effector → proximal.

  • huunguyenSat May 30 2026 21:48:09 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    Exactly right

  • vankhuenguyenMon Jun 01 2026 14:30:01 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 yes it's done, full dataset is uploaded which is the phase 2 output

  • vankhuenguyenMon Jun 01 2026 14:32:48 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 Yes, it covers the entire video length matched by video_id. If HRNet detected no human, it outputs [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]. MotionBERT might smooth it slightly across empty frames due to its temporal window, but in my next step (Phase 3 Kinematics), I explicitly hunt down these [0,0,0] pelvis frames and turn the entire frame into NaN so we can drop them completely.

  • vankhuenguyenMon Jun 01 2026 14:33:59 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 The shape is (T, 17, 3) stored as raw float32 bytes: - T: Total frames in the video. - 17: Number of joints (Human3.6M format, 0 is Pelvis, 16 is R_Wrist). - 3: X, Y, Z coordinates in meters (camera-relative).

  • vankhuenguyenMon Jun 01 2026 14:37:30 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • vankhuenguyenMon Jun 01 2026 14:44:32 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 <@827568132444651560> I just realize that before when I do tokenization for cosmos, avc-lm and seed2, I used ffmpeg to hardcode the fps to 30fps regardless of their initial fps

  • vankhuenguyenMon Jun 01 2026 14:44:54 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    but for this .npy file, the fps is the initial fps not 30 fps

  • vankhuenguyenMon Jun 01 2026 14:49:50 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @huunguyen#0 code to convert: ```python import numpy as np from datasets import load_dataset ds = load_dataset("EmpathicRobotics/finevideo-3d-pose", split="train", streaming=True) for row in ds: # 1. Load poses: shape (T, 17, 3) poses = np.frombuffer(row["joints_3d"], dtype=np.float32).reshape(row["num_frames"], 17, 3) # 2. Get true FPS (I will add this column soon, assuming 30 for now) fps = 30.0 # 3. Create time column t: shape (T, 1) t = np.arange(row["num_frames"])[:, None] / fps # 4. Interleave to shape (T, 17*3 + 1) = (T, 52) xyzt_interleaved = np.concatenate([poses.reshape(row["num_frames"], -1), t], axis=1) break ```

  • vankhuenguyenMon Jun 01 2026 15:03:24 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • debbieyWed Jun 10 2026 00:14:58 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • debbieyWed Jun 10 2026 00:26:48 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    HumanML3D — github.com/EricGuo5513/HumanML3D The go-to for text-labeled diverse motion. Covers a broad spectrum of movements including everyday behaviors like walking or jumping, athletic activities such as swimming and karate, acrobatic skills like cartwheels, and performance-oriented motions such as dancing. It's built on AMASS + HumanAct12. License: research/academic use only. arxiv AMASS — amass.is.tue.mpg.de Contains SMPL poses spanning 346 subjects and 11,451 motions across a diverse range of activities including locomotion, sports, dance, martial arts, and everyday actions. Free for research; commercial use requires emailing ps-license@tue.mpg.de. arxivMpg CIMI4D — Climbing-specific A CVPR 2023 dataset specifically for climbing motions with full 3D scene interaction — great if you need complex wall/body interaction data beyond floor-based movement. MoVi (part of AMASS) 90 actors (60 women, 30 men) performing 20 predefined everyday actions and sports movements, plus one self-chosen movement. Good body diversity. nih BONES Studio — bones.studio/datasets 700+ hours of labeled 3D animations performed by 170+ physically diverse performers including professionals like stuntmen, soldiers, and dancers. Optical mocap at 120fps. Open source for academic use and qualifying startups. More diverse than most — worth looking at if you need professional-grade motion with body diversity. Bones Studio

  • huunguyenSun Jun 14 2026 21:22:25 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    @andreaskoepf#0 <@719599526448463933> is this accurate: ```The goal of this initiative is to update and refine the dataset hosted at [`EmpathicRobotics/FineVideo-VLA-flattened`](https://www.google.com/search?q=%5Bhttps://huggingface.co/datasets/EmpathicRobotics/FineVideo-VLA-flattened%5D(https://huggingface.co/datasets/EmpathicRobotics/FineVideo-VLA-flattened)) to train an **Omni-VLA (Vision-Language-Action)** model. The pipeline will ingest real-world human motion capture (mocap) data, actual camera feeds, and simulated environments to predict continuous robot movements for Reinforcement Learning (RL) in simulation and smooth, real-world deployment via a downstream flow-matching diffuser.```

    @vankhuenguyen#0 <@631456778432937986> please summarize what you did. And can you copy a bit of the data here?

  • vankhuenguyenFri Jun 19 2026 19:23:21 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

  • vankhuenguyenFri Jun 19 2026 19:25:37 GMT+0700 (Indochina Time)

    - Phase 3 & 4 (Filtering): Continued from Phase 2.5 (30fps joints). I ran the kinematics and YOLO filtering to strictly clean out any windows with missing joints (NaNs). - Phase 5b (XYZT Tokens - NO PCHIP): Just to clarify, I have completely removed all the old PCHIP/spline logic. It now strictly produces frame-by-frame, self-describing tokens like ... The absolute time t is saved separately in the .npy arrays for the simulation side. (PLEASE check if this is right or your idea is different) - Merge & Flattening: I merged the new XYZT agent tokens with our existing FineVideo dataset (cosmos, seed2, avc_lm) using the time alignment. I used your flatten.py script but had to modify it slightly: - Fixed the token parsing logic so it doesn't break our new tags. - Regarding the "Drop rate": Before flattening, the image tokens (avc_lm) were massively outnumbering the action tokens (~373x more). So, I applied a "drop rate" (dropping ~99% of avc_lm and 90% of cosmos during flattening) to bring all modalities into a balanced ratio. Final Output: Everything is flattened and pushed to the HF datasets: - EmpathicRobotics/FineVideo-VLA-flattened: Flattened Megatron-LM JSONL (final output, ready for pretraining) - EmpathicRobotics/FineVideo-VLA-Agent: Per-joint XYZT merged dataset (pre-flattening, hierarchical)
